etymology.description: 玫(玫) (méi, "rose gemstone; beautiful small stone") has a historical association with something small, precious, and beautiful, similar to a gemstone. 瑰(瑰) (guī, "rare; precious; exceptional") emphasizes uniqueness and value, often referring to something remarkable or extraordinary. Together, 玫瑰(玫瑰) (méiguī, "rose") evokes the image of a precious and exceptionally beautiful flower.
etymology.interpretation: 玫(玫) (méi, "rose gemstone; beautiful small stone") focuses on beauty and preciousness, suggesting something delicate and charming like a gemstone. 瑰(瑰) (guī, "rare; precious; exceptional") highlights the unique and extraordinary qualities of the rose, marking it as a rare flower of great beauty. The combination implies an exquisite, highly valued flower—like a 'gem among flowers', hence the concept of a rose.

examples.chinese: 玫瑰花开得很美(玫瑰花開得很美)。
examples.pinyin: Méi guī huā kāi de hěn měi.

examples.chinese: 她收到了一束红玫瑰(她收到了一束紅玫瑰)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shōu dào le yī shù hóng méi guī.
examples.english: She received a bouquet of red roses.

examples.chinese: 玫瑰象征爱情(玫瑰象徵愛情)。
examples.pinyin: Méi guī xiàng zhēng ài qíng.
